title: Palindromic Substrings
description:
## Palindromic Substrings

Given a string `s`, return the number of substrings that are palindromes. A palindrome reads the same forwards and backwards. Single characters always count as palindromes.

**Constraints:**
- `1 <= s.length <= 1000`
- `s` consists of lowercase English letters.

exampleCases.explanation: a, b, b, a, bb, abba

hiddenCases.explanation: r,a,c,e,c,a,r + aca(×1) + aceca + racecar = 7 singles + cec,aceca,racecar = 10
C(5,2) = 10 — all substrings of all same chars are palindromes

solutions.label: Expand Around Center — O(n²)
solutions.explanation: Every palindrome expands from a center. For each index, expand outward for both odd-length palindromes (single center) and even-length palindromes (pair center), counting every valid expansion. This visits each palindrome exactly once.

solutions.label: DP — O(n²)
solutions.explanation: Build a 2D DP table: `dp[i][j]` is True iff `s[i..j]` is a palindrome. Base cases: every single character and every matching adjacent pair. For lengths ≥ 3: `dp[i][j]` is True if outer characters match and `dp[i+1][j-1]` is True. Count every True entry. Uses O(n²) space vs O(1) for the expand-around-center approach.

solutions.label: Expand Around Center — O(n²)
solutions.explanation: Every palindrome expands from a center. For each index, expand outward for both odd-length and even-length palindromes, counting every valid expansion.

hints: Every palindrome has a center — a single character for odd-length or a matching pair for even-length.
Try expanding outward from each center: every successful expansion is a new palindromic substring to count.
